tool.name: chainStitchId
tool.description: join chain fragments that share the same chain id

param.name: input
param.label: Input chain file
param.help: UCSC chain-format alignment. Fragments carrying the same chain id are joined into single chains.

tool.help:
**chainStitchId** — join chain fragments that share the same chain id.
A UCSC *chain* file describes gapped pairwise alignments between a target and a query sequence. During chaining workflows a single logical chain can end up split across several records that all carry the **same chain id** (for example after `chainNet` / `netChainSubset` style processing). `chainStitchId` reads the input chain file and re-joins every set of records that share an id into a single chain, writing the stitched result to a new chain file.
``` chainStitchId in.chain out.chain ```
-----
**Inputs**
- `Input chain file` — alignments in UCSC chain format.
**Outputs**
- `Stitched chains` — chain file in which fragments with identical ids have been merged.
-----
chainStitchId is part of Jim Kent's UCSC Genome Browser utilities (the chain/net alignment toolkit). It is the one chain utility not yet available through the IUC tool collection.
